description_long: This object is a dark, elongated stone with a roughly pointed end and a broader, rounded opposite side. Its surface is irregular and uneven, featuring visible chipping and flaking, suggesting it may have been shaped or weathered over time. The stone is predominantly a deep charcoal or black color, with subtle variations and occasional lighter specks. On one side, there are faint markings or inscriptions in white and pink, possibly catalog numbers or identifiers. The overall texture appears coarse and matte, with no visible gloss or polish. Its size and form suggest it could serve as a natural obstacle or decorative element in an urban landscape.
description: A dark, elongated stone with a rough, chipped surface and subtle color variations, featuring faint markings on one side.
description_view_0: The stone appears broad and pointed at one end, with a rough, uneven surface and a matte finish.
description_view_1: Seen from the narrow edge, the stone looks thin and elongated, emphasizing its tapered profile.
description_view_2: This side displays faint white and pink markings, with the stone's full length and irregular surface visible.
description_view_3: Similar to view 1, the stone is seen edge-on, highlighting its slender, elongated shape.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or loose obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular chipping and flaking with faint, hand-applied markings; no natural veining or regular pattern.
attribute_weathering: Surface is rough and matte, with signs of chipping but little evidence of extensive weathering or lichen growth.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically contrasts with smoother, man-made surfaces but may blend with other natural stones or landscaping elements.
